title: allium kollmannianum
intro: Allium kollmannianum is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, commonly known as the Kollmann's onion or mountain garlic. It is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, where it grows in rocky and alpine meadows. Allium kollmannianum is valued for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system.
